samples.text: Obs. III. There is a goodness and excellency in this heavenly gift, which may be tasted or experienced in some measure by such as never receive them, in their life, power, and efficacy. They may taste,—(1.) Of the word in its truth, not its power; (2.) Of the

1. Its communication or application unto the earth,—it falls upon it; 2. An especial adjunct thereof in its frequency,—it falls often on it; 3. By that reception which the earth is naturally fitted and suited to give unto it,—it drinketh it in.
